title: Cloudflare Workers Builds was deploying every successful PR-branch build to production, not just merges to main; the misconfiguration ran undetected for the full duration of PRs #33, #34, and #40 before being surfaced by the migration-mismatch failure of PR #41
content: Direct evidence from the GitHub check-run timeline for PR #40 and the failed PR #41 build log: PR #40 had 5 commits (080238e, bd061a1, d2158cf, 00fe714, 798af9a) over 33 minutes (03:17Z-03:50Z), each producing a Workers Build that ran 'Executing user deploy command: npx wrangler deploy' (the production deploy command) instead of 'npx wrangler versions upload' (the preview default per Cloudflare docs). Each successful build replaced production. PR #40 was not merged to main until 04:37Z — 47 minutes after its first successful build. Slice 5 (PR #41) build at 03:33Z failed with 'Cannot apply new-class migration to class ConversationDO that is already depended on by existing Durable Objects [code: 10074]' because production already had the v3 migration applied (from PR #40's PR-branch deploy at 03:24Z), but Slice 5's branch wrangler.toml only declared v1 and v2. The v3 migration entry was only on PR #40's branch, not on main. Production drifted from the source of truth for ~73 minutes. Live probe at 03:50Z confirmed production was running the SDK-backed wrapper from PR #40's HEAD (session ID format was 64-char hex per SDK convention, not the handroll's 'mcps_<rand>.<b64>.<b64>' format; capabilities included listChanged:true on resources/tools per SDK defaults; prompts and logging capabilities both absent because the McpAgent subclass did not register them).

title: The default Workers Builds behavior per Cloudflare docs is safe; the misconfiguration was an active opt-in or override; root cause traces to either the production-branch setting being too permissive (matching PR branches) or the non-production-deploy-command being customized away from the preview default
content: Per Cloudflare docs (workers/ci-cd/builds/build-branches and workers/ci-cd/builds/configuration): the production branch defaults to the repo's default branch (main); only commits to that branch run the deploy command (default 'npx wrangler deploy'); non-production branches require explicit opt-in via 'Builds for non-production branches' checkbox AND default to 'npx wrangler versions upload' which creates a preview version without promoting to production. The build log evidence — 'Executing user deploy command: npx wrangler deploy' on PR-branch commits — proves both opt-ins were active AND the non-production deploy command was either explicitly set to 'npx wrangler deploy' OR the production branch glob was set to match all branches. Cannot determine which from CF API alone (Workers Builds settings are not exposed via the MCP this session has access to); operator inspection of dashboard required for definitive root cause. Operator updated production branch to 'main' at ~04:42Z; this addresses the production-branch-glob hypothesis but does not by itself address the non-production-deploy-command-override hypothesis.

title: Production-deploy-from-PR-branch is a class of risk distinct from the more common deploy-on-merge-to-main pattern; it inverts code review (PR builds bypass the review gate) and creates source-of-truth drift between main and the running worker; the failure mode hides cleanly because every build "succeeds" — the cost surfaces as cross-PR migration mismatches like the one PR #41 hit
content: The reason this went undetected for three PRs (#33, #34, #40) is that none of them produced a migration mismatch within their own scope: #33 introduced v1+v2 fresh; #34 modified the existing handroll without changing migrations; PR #40's first build introduced v3 to production cleanly because main already had v1+v2. The migration-mismatch failure was specifically a consequence of TWO PRs being open simultaneously where the second PR (#41) branched off main BEFORE the first PR (#40) had merged. This is a normal collaboration pattern; it is the misconfiguration that turned it into a deploy-blocking failure. Reviewer inversion is a more serious concern than the migration block: the SDK rewrite (PR #40) was deployed to production at 03:24Z and ran for 73 minutes before any operator review of the merge. Upstream canon klappy://docs/decisions/D0001 ("prod Branch Is Production") records the same principle from a prior incident in klappy.dev where agent experiments on main accidentally nuked production; the resolution there was to make 'prod' the deploy branch and reduce 'main' to an experiment ledger. AMS uses a different model (main IS the production deploy branch per CF Workers Builds default), so D0001's specific solution does not transfer, but the underlying principle — production deploys must be gated by an explicit promotion event, not by every branch push — is identical and was violated here. Code that survived bug-bot review and that operator merged after gate-checking was code that had already been live in production. Future regressions in PR-branch builds would similarly deploy live before any merge gate. The fix (production branch = main) restores the gate for the production-branch-glob root cause; non-production deploy command verification remains the standing item.

title: Production branch setting in Workers Builds was changed from prior misconfigured value to 'main' by operator at ~04:42Z; standing decision: production deploys flow only from main, PR builds produce preview URLs only via 'npx wrangler versions upload'
content: Operator action: 'Updated to main now' acknowledged at 04:42Z. This is the necessary half of the fix but verification that it is sufficient remains pending. The non-production deploy command must also be 'npx wrangler versions upload' (the CF default) and not customized to 'npx wrangler deploy'. Operator dashboard inspection or a test PR-branch push (which would log 'Executing user deploy command: npx wrangler versions upload' if correctly configured, or 'Executing user deploy command: npx wrangler deploy' if still misconfigured) would confirm. The standing decision binds future PRs: any PR-branch push should produce only a preview deployment, and production should only update on push to main. Verification gate: the next PR-branch push (PR #41 force-rebase, or any subsequent PR) must show the preview deploy command in the build log; if it shows the production deploy command, the second fix is required.

title: Workers Builds production-branch setting MUST be 'main'; non-production-branch deploy command MUST be 'npx wrangler versions upload' (or any other command that does not execute 'wrangler deploy' against the production environment); any customization to either setting requires explicit acknowledgment and a journal entry recording why; future onboarding of new Workers projects to this account must verify these settings before merging the first PR
content: The constraint exists because the failure mode (PR-branch deploys to production) is silent — every build "succeeds" — and creates source-of-truth drift between main and the live worker that only surfaces under specific conditions (concurrent PRs, migration changes, SessionDO key rotation, etc). Verification is cheap (one dashboard check per project, one log-line check per PR push); recovery from a missed verification is expensive (cross-PR migration mismatches, reviewer-inversion risk, potential live regression from un-reviewed code). The standing instruction is preventive: verify before the first PR push, not reactively after a failure. This constraint is project-meta (about CF Workers Builds configuration), not about AMS canon; it is recorded in the journal rather than canon proper because it does not constrain the AMS protocol or wire surface. If Anthropic operators add other Workers projects to this account in the future, this journal entry serves as the operational reference.

title: Verification of the non-production deploy command is the immediate next step; the most efficient verification is to push an empty commit to any open PR branch (PR #41 is the natural candidate) and read the resulting build log; standing instructions for any future Workers Builds onboarding are recorded in the Constraint row above
content: Verification options: (a) operator inspects dashboard at Workers > agent-messaging-service > Settings > Build > Deploy command and Non-production branch deploy command — should read 'npx wrangler deploy' and 'npx wrangler versions upload' respectively; (b) Claude pushes an empty commit to PR #41's branch and inspects the resulting CF build log for 'Executing user deploy command: <which>'; (c) operator merges PR #41 to main, which exercises only the happy path (main pushes deploy to production) and does not isolate whether PR-branch deploys are still happening. Option (a) is fastest and most authoritative; option (b) is verifiable from this conversation without operator intervention; option (c) provides no diagnostic signal about the bug. The slice 6 PR is canon-only (this journal entry); it does not include any code changes or any of the verification options. Verification path is operator-led for option (a) or Claude-led for option (b); choose at next operator turn. Once verified, this entry can be amended with an additional H row documenting the resolution.
